tool_args.label: Triborough Bridge and Tunnel Authority
tool_args.raw_text: The legal name of MTA Bridges and Tunnels is Triborough Bridge and Tunnel Authority

tool_args.label: Metropolitan Transportation Authority
tool_args.raw_text: Metropolitan Transportation Authority's ("MTA") Proposed Financial Plan 2025-2028

tool_args.label: Paul L. Friman
tool_args.raw_text: Department Law Department Head Name Paul L. Friman

tool_args.label: Board Authorized Staff to Proceed with Crossing Charge Increase Steps
tool_args.raw_text: On December 18, 2024, the Board authorized Authority staff to proceed with the steps necessary to consider, but not implement, a proposed new Crossing Charge structure

payload.description: Board authorized TBTA staff to proceed with steps necessary to consider proposed new Crossing Charge structure

tool_args.label: Notice of Proposed Crossing Charge Increases Published in State Register
tool_args.raw_text: Notice of the proposed changes was published in the State Register on July 16, 2025, soliciting public comments.

payload.description: Notice of proposed Crossing Charge increases published in the New York State Register soliciting public comments

tool_args.label: Board Adopted Resolution for Environmental/Just and Reasonable Determination
tool_args.raw_text: September 30, 2025 ... the Board, by resolution adopted September 30, 2025, found that the proposed Crossing Charge increases ... will have no significant adverse effects on the environment

payload.description: Board adopted Resolution finding proposed Crossing Charge increases have no significant adverse environmental effects, adopting Negative Declaration, and finding increases just and reasonable

tool_args.label: Board Adopted Resolution Repealing Prior and Adopting New Crossing Charge Schedule
tool_args.raw_text: RESOLVED, that ... the new Crossing Charge Schedule ... providing for certain increases in the Crossing Charge structure of the Authority, as set forth in the attachment to this resolution, shall be and hereby is adopted to be implemented on or about January 4, 2026

payload.description: Board adopted Resolution repealing prior Crossing Charge Schedule and adopting new Schedule with increases, to be implemented on or about January 4, 2026

tool_args.label: New Crossing Charges Planned Implementation
tool_args.raw_text: the new Crossing Charge Schedule ... adopted to be implemented on or about January 4, 2026

payload.description: New Crossing Charge Schedule planned for implementation

tool_args.label: No Significant Adverse Environmental Impacts Found
tool_args.raw_text: That analysis concluded that no significant adverse impacts to the environment would arise at any Authority facility or along toll-free diversion routes from such a toll increase.

payload.claim_text: The proposed Crossing Charge increases will have no significant adverse effects on the environment at any Authority facility or along toll-free diversion routes.
payload.subject: Proposed Crossing Charge increases

tool_args.label: Crossing Charges Found Just and Reasonable
tool_args.raw_text: RESOLVED, that the Crossing Charges for use of the facilities of the Authority, as increased by the amounts in the new Crossing Charge Schedule, are hereby found to be 'just and reasonable.'

payload.claim_text: The Crossing Charges for use of the facilities of the Authority, as increased by the amounts in the new Crossing Charge Schedule, are hereby found to be 'just and reasonable.'
payload.subject: Crossing Charge increases

tool_args.label: Financial Plan Contemplates Toll Increases
tool_args.raw_text: The Metropolitan Transportation Authority's ("MTA") Proposed Financial Plan 2025-2028, presented at the November 2024 Board meeting, contemplates implementation of increased tolls and fares, to achieve budgeted revenue targets.

payload.claim_text: The MTA's Proposed Financial Plan 2025-2028 contemplates implementation of increased tolls and fares to achieve budgeted revenue targets.
payload.subject: MTA Proposed Financial Plan 2025-2028
